intro i
  -- For each index `i`, we need to check whether the corresponding measure is a probability measure.
  -- If `i.val.1 = i.val.2`, then the measure is `ν`, which is a probability measure by assumption.
  -- If `i.val.1 ≠ i.val.2`, then the measure is `μ`, which is also a probability measure by assumption.
  by_cases h : (i : Index n).val.1 = (i : Index n).val.2
  · -- Case: i.val.1 = i.val.2
    simp [h]
    <;> exact inferInstance
  · -- Case: i.val.1 ≠ i.val.2

    <;> exact inferInstance
